title: Coffee on Polyester
timeEstimate: 20–40 minutes; cold water + Dawn + peroxide if needed
stainChemistry: Coffee = melanoidins + chlorogenic acid + tannin polyphenols. On polyester, tannins bond through hydrogen bonding to the fiber, but polyester is OLEOPHILIC — it grabs and holds oil tenaciously. Coffee with cream adds milk casein + butterfat; on polyester, the butterfat is the harder fraction (oleophilic affinity). Tannin rules apply: NO alkaline cleaners (OxiClean, baking soda, ammonia) — turn tannin permanently darker. NO hot water — cross-links tannin AND can permanently set the polymer film of coffee around polyester fibers. Polyester tolerates standard tannin chemistry (cold water, Dawn, 3% peroxide) well.
whyThisWorks: Cold water + Dawn lifts the fat fraction (oleophilic polyester needs Dawn). 3% peroxide on a damp cloth oxidizes residual tannin. Vinegar rinse keeps tannin on the lighter (acid) side of the color equilibrium. Polyester doesn't felt or denature — tolerates the protocol. The honest cap: polyester yellows under heat exposure, so any residual coffee + dryer = permanent.
defaultAssumption: Polyester garment / blend / workout wear. Fresh coffee = quick win. Hot-dried coffee = hard. Coffee with cream needs the protein step (warm enzyme pretreat at <100°F) before peroxide.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Flip garment inside out. Run cold water through the BACK of the stain into the sink. Cold pulls fresh tannin out the way it came in.
Work Dawn into the spot with a soft toothbrush. Polyester is oleophilic — Dawn lifts butterfat (if cream was added) and emulsifies coffee residue. Cold rinse.

spottingProtocol.equipment: OxiClean Versatile / Biz in WARM (<100°F) water
spottingProtocol.instruction: If coffee had cream: enzyme pretreat in WARM water — bath temperature, not hot. Damp cloth on spot 15 min. Skip for black coffee.
Hidden-area test if dyed polyester. 3% peroxide on damp cloth, 5–10 min. Don't mix with ammonia / bleach / alkaline.

spottingProtocol.equipment: 1 tbsp vinegar + 1 cup cool water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Vinegar + water rinse. Neutralizes peroxide + keeps tannin light.
Cool wash per care label (NOT hot — heat sets coffee tannin AND yellows polyester over time). Air-dry. Inspect BEFORE dryer — heat permanently sets residual coffee on polyester.

steps: 3. Enzyme pretreat WARM (not hot) for cream-coffee, 15 min.
6. Cool wash + air-dry. Inspect before dryer.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold throughout for tannin; warm (<100°F) for enzyme; never hot wash
warnings: NEVER hot water — sets coffee tannin + yellows polyester
NEVER alkaline cleaners on tannin — darkens permanently
NEVER chlorine bleach on polyester — yellows fiber
NEVER hot dryer on residual coffee — sets permanently
Polyester is oleophilic — Dawn step is mandatory for cream-coffee

homeSolutions.instruction: Coffee on polyester is one of the easier high-frequency stains IF you follow two rules: (1) NO alkaline cleaners (no OxiClean, no baking soda, no ammonia) — they turn tannin DARKER permanently, and (2) NO hot water (no hot wash, no hot dryer until the stain is fully gone — heat sets coffee + yellows polyester).
Flip the garment inside out. Run cold tap water through the BACK of the stain for 1–2 minutes. Cold pulls fresh coffee out the way it came in — the most effective fast move.

Work a drop of Dawn dish soap into the wet stain with a soft toothbrush. Polyester is oleophilic (loves oil), so Dawn is essential — especially if the coffee had cream (butterfat). 5 minutes dwell. Rinse cool.

homeSolutions.agent: Cream-Coffee? Enzyme Pretreat in Warm (Not Hot) Water
homeSolutions.instruction: If the coffee had cream, dissolve OxiClean Versatile or Biz in WARM water — bath temperature, under 100°F. NOT hot. Apply to the spot for 15 minutes. The enzyme digests milk casein. Skip for black coffee.
Dab 3% drugstore peroxide directly on residual stain, 5–10 minutes. Hidden-area test first on dyed polyester. Skip on chlorine-bleach-treated polyester (already yellowed).
Mix 1 tablespoon white vinegar + 1 cup cool water. Apply for 5 minutes. Neutralizes peroxide + keeps any residual tannin in its lighter (acid) color. Rinse cool.

Wash on COOL per care label — NOT hot. Air-dry. Inspect BEFORE the dryer. ANY residual coffee + dryer heat = permanent stain on polyester. Once you can confirm the stain is fully gone in daylight, normal dryer is fine.

steps: 1. NO alkaline cleaners. NO hot water until clear.
5. 3% peroxide damp cloth, 5–10 min, hidden test on dyed.
7. Cool wash + AIR-DRY. Inspect BEFORE dryer.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if hot dryer was used on residual coffee (likely permanent), if alkaline was applied (tannin locked), or if the stain has been there for weeks with heat exposure.
warnings: NEVER hot water — sets coffee + yellows polyester
NEVER alkaline cleaners on tannin — darkens permanently
NEVER chlorine bleach on polyester — yellows fiber
NEVER hot dryer until fully clear — heat sets residual coffee
NEVER mix peroxide with bleach / ammonia / alkaline

neverDo: Never hot water on coffee — sets tannin
Never OxiClean / baking soda / ammonia — darkens tannin
Never chlorine bleach on polyester — yellows fiber
Never hot dryer until stain is fully clear
Never mix peroxide with bleach / ammonia
fiberSensitivities: Polyester is oleophilic — Dawn step matters for cream-coffee
Polyester yellows under heat over time
Polyester tolerates 3% peroxide on dyed (with hidden test)
Polyester is heat-sensitive at high temps — air-dry safer

materialWarnings: No hot water — sets coffee + yellows polyester
Air-dry until fully clear; dryer heat permanent
Polyester loves oil — Dawn is mandatory for cream-coffee

escalation.when: Hot dryer used on residual coffee, alkaline cleaner applied previously, set-in stain weeks old with heat exposure, or polyester is delicate fabric (chiffon-style polyester / peau de soie).
escalation.whatToTell: "Coffee on polyester, [black / with cream], age [hours / days], home-treated with cold + Dawn + 3% peroxide + vinegar + cool wash. No hot water / no alkaline / no chlorine bleach. [Residual brown / hot-dried / weeks-old]."

contextQuestions: How long ago — fresh / overnight / older?
Has it been through a hot wash or dryer?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Cream coffee adds protein step (warm enzyme). Hot-dried coffee = often permanent. Delicate polyester (chiffon-style) = treat closer to silk protocol. Pre-applied OxiClean = tannin darkened permanently.
